behaviors.description: Reject webhook with mismatched `X-Signature-256` and do not forward body
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handoff between signature-verification logic and enqueue path (handler + verifier + queue adapter). `MOCK-MISS`: a pure unit test of verifier alone misses accidental forwarding. `REAL-INFRA` rejected: behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real network/OS timing. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP` disqualified by behavior text (HMAC check, no AI steps).
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real request-routing + signature middleware + queue adapter wired in-process; send body/header mismatch; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue write method/store receives zero events.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200
behaviors.justification: This is explicit cross-module ordering/state propagation (HTTP ack depends on queue write completion), so Unit is too low per integration-handoff escalator. `REAL-INFRA` considered and rejected: ordering bug is reproducible with in-process queue implementation that can delay/confirm commit; no real OS/network needed by behavior text. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a real local persistent-queue module (e.g., sqlite/file-backed in-process). Instrument commit completion timestamp and HTTP response timestamp; assert commit occurs first, then 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary between intake handler and async downstream processor. Unit-only logic test could miss accidental await/join in orchestration. `System` rejected: behavior text requires non-blocking orchestration, not real network/OS primitives. `Agentic`/`Workflow` disqualified (no model decisions).
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process app wiring with a downstream consumer stub that blocks for a long duration; send valid webhook and assert response returns promptly while processor is still running.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds under concurrent slow downstream load
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly wall-clock SLO under concurrency. `REAL-INFRA` and `MOCK-HIDE`: in-process fakes can hide event-loop saturation, socket/backpressure, and server scheduling effects that cause real timeout breaches. Requires real HTTP server/subprocess/runtime timing. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Start the actual Node service, generate concurrent valid webhook traffic while prior events trigger intentionally slow processing, and measure end-to-end HTTP latency at the client; assert each response is <=3s.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not written to queue again
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is state handoff across handler/idempotency store/queue write path; Unit is too low. `System` considered and rejected: behavior text is about write suppression by ID, which can be reproduced with local wired modules and persistent store fixture. No AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real dedupe + queue modules against local persistence; send same event ID twice; assert first request enqueues once and second causes no additional queue record.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and include `Retry-After`
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is contract mapping from queue-capacity signal to HTTP response semantics (status + header), i.e., module interaction. `System` rejected: exact failure can be reproduced by real queue module reaching capacity in-process; no real network/OS condition required by behavior wording. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue capacity small in integration fixture, fill it, then submit one more webhook through handler; assert 503 and presence/value format of `Retry-After`, and no extra queue write.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets real queue component semantics (persistence + capacity), not a single pure function. Needs queue module with real local storage wiring; Unit fake could miss enforcement bugs. `System` considered: upgrade only if external OS/subprocess/network is required; behavior text says persistent queue but not necessarily external service, so Integration remains minimum with local DB/filesystem backend. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate actual persistent queue backend used by service with low configured capacity, enqueue up to limit, then one extra; assert overflow operation returns/reports rejection and stored depth never exceeds limit across restart/readback.
